displayTitle: Surface Waves, Lamb Waves, Echo vs Shadow

beats.narration: Two waves live at boundaries: surface, and plate. And any wave works two ways: echo, or shadow.

payload.title: WAVES ON THE EDGE
payload.sub: Two waves that live at boundaries, two ways to use them.

beats.narration: What survives past the second critical angle grips the surface: a Rayleigh wave, energy within about one wavelength. Surface cracks echo; a fingertip absorbs it.

payload.heading: The Rayleigh wave: a skin inspector
payload.note: ANYTHING ON THE SURFACE IS IN THE TEST

beats.narration: Only a few wavelengths thick, the whole plate vibrates: Lamb waves. Symmetric modes breathe, asymmetric flex, and velocity tracks frequency times thickness: dispersion.

payload.heading: Lamb waves: the whole plate vibrates

beats.narration: Pulse echo: one probe sends and listens; arrival time gives depth, from one side. Through transmission: two aligned probes; a flaw's shadow drops the signal.

payload.heading: Pulse echo vs. through-transmission
payload.note: ONE PROBE, DEPTH · TWO PROBES, SHADOW

beats.narration: Echo gives depth from one side. Shadow gives go or no go, and needs two sides. Skin, sheet, depth, shadow: four tools, four jobs.

payload.sub: Surface, Lamb, pulse echo, through transmission.
